description: Negative fuel quantity flagged by the deterministic sign-check rule.

template_vars.validation_errors: quantity: Negative quantities are not permitted.

expected_response.explanation: The recorded fuel quantity is -500,00, which the deterministic validator rejects because negative quantities are not permitted; this is most likely a sign error introduced when the source system exported a credit/return transaction as a plain purchase row.
expected_response.suggested_correction: Confirm with the source system whether this was a return or credit, and re-enter the row with the correct sign or as a separate adjustment entry.

description: Unparseable electricity consumption value flagged by the deterministic numeric-parse rule.

template_vars.validation_errors: quantity: Unable to parse numeric value.

expected_response.explanation: The Consumption field contains the text 'N/A' instead of a number, which the deterministic validator cannot parse as a quantity; this usually means the utility meter read was missing or unavailable when this bill was exported.
expected_response.suggested_correction: Obtain the actual meter read for HQ-London's February 2026 billing period from the utility provider and re-enter it in place of 'N/A'.

description: Unrecognized unit code flagged by the deterministic unit-lookup rule -- a regression case covering a different validation error type than the quantity-based cases above.

template_vars.validation_errors: unit: Unknown unit code 'XYZ' is not a recognized SAP unit.

expected_response.explanation: The unit code 'XYZ' is not among the recognized SAP fuel units (e.g. L, KG, GAL), which the deterministic validator rejects; this is most likely a typo or an export-mapping error in the source system's unit-of-measure field.
expected_response.suggested_correction: Check the source system's unit-of-measure configuration for this material and re-enter the row with a recognized unit code such as L or KG.
